content_en:
Various Ahaadeeth About The Dajjaal

The hadith Of Abu Bakr As-Siddeeq -

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 107

like shields, will follow him.’” (Ahmad) Ibn Maajah and AtTirmidhee related it from Rooh ibn ‘Ubaadah, and At-Tirmidhee said, “Hasan sahih.”

The hadith Of ‘Alee Ibn Abee Taalib 4

‘Alee ts said, “We mentioned the Dajjaal in the presence of the Prophet @ as he was sleeping. He woke up, red in color, and he said, “Other than that do I fear more upon you.” (Ahmad)

The hadith Of Sa’ad Ibn Abee Waqqaas 4

Sa’ad related from Maalik from his father from his father, that the Messenger of Allah 2% said, “Every single Prophet described the Dajjaal to his nation, and I will describe a quality of his that no one before me has described (regarding him). He is indeed one-eyed, and Allah ‘Azza wa-Jall (to Him belongs Might and Majesty) is not one-eyed.” (Ahmad)

The hadith Of Abu ‘Ubaidah Ibn Al-Jarraah 2

Abu ‘Ubaidah ibn Al-Jarraah related that he heard the Messenger of Allah % say, “Indeed, there was never a Prophet except that he warned his people about the Dafjaal, and I am warning you about him.” Abu ‘Ubaidah said, “The Messenger of Allah #% then described him for us and said, ‘Perhaps some who see (me) and hear my speech will reach him (i.e., will be alive when he comes out).”" The Companions 4 said, “O Messenger of Allah, how will our hearts be at that time?” He #% said, “The same - as today - or better.” It is related by At-Tirmidhee, who said that it is Hasan. Abu Daawood related it as well, and Ahmad related a part of it.

‘Abdullah Ibn Khabbaab listened to Ubai Ibn Ka’ab & as he was relating from the Messenger of Allah #. When the Dajjaal was mentioned in his presence, he said, “It is as if one of his two eyes is glass. And seek refuge in Allah from the punishment of the grave.” (Ahmad)

108 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

indeed the Seal of 1000 or more (Prophets). No followed Prophet was sent except that he warned his nation about the Dajjaal, and a matter has indeed been made clear to me, which has not hitherto been made clear to anyone. He is one-eyed and indeed your Lord is not one-eyed. It is his right eye that is ‘Aouraa (defected); it is not concealed but rather protrudes abnormally, as if it is phlegm on a plastered wall. And his left eye is like a glistening star. With him is every language and with him is a picture of Paradise, with greenery; and in it water flows; and (with him is) a picture of the Fire, black and giving off smoke.” (Ahmad)

A hadith Related From Anas Ibn Maalik 4

Anas ibn Maalik .% related that the Messenger of Allah 2% said, “The Dajjaal will come and step on every part of the earth except for Makkah and Al-Madeenah. He will come to Al-Madeenah and find at each of its mountain-passes rows of angels. Then he will go the side of a land that is barren and salty, and he will strike his tent (or place where he is seated). Al-Madeenah will shake three times, and every male and female hypocrite will come out of it and go to him.” (Ahmad) Muslim related it as well, but through another chain.

Another Chain From Anas

Anas 4 related that the Prophet ## said, “Indeed the Dajjaal is oneeyed, from the left (eye), over which is coarse skin. Written between his eyes is Kafara (the past-tense verb for disbelief) or Kaafir (cisbelief)."” (Ahmad) This narration fulfills the conditions of Bukhaaree and Muslim.

Another Chain From Anas

Anas ibn Maalik . related that the Messenger of Allah #¥ said, “The Dajjaal will come out from the Jews of Asbahaan; with him are 70,000 Jews, and upon them are crowns.” (Ahmad) And Anas related that the Messenger of Allah x said, “The Dajjaal has an eye that is effaced, and between his eyes is written Kaafir (disbeliever).”” Then the Prophet # spoke the letters (of Kaafir): Ka-Fa-Ra. He #¢ then said, “Every Muslim will read it.” (Ahmad) And in another narration, Anas ibn Maalik 4s related that the Messenger of Allah 2 said, “The Dajjaal is one-eyed, and indeed, your Lord is not oneeyed. Written between the eyes of the Dajjaal is Kaafir, which every

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations ‘109

believer will read, regardless of whether he is literate or illiterate.” (Almad) Muslim related it as well, but through another chain.

Yet Another Chain From Anas 4

Anas .& related that the Messenger of Allah 2% said, “No Prophet ‘was sent except that he warned his nation about the One-Eyed Liar; except he is indeed one-eyed, and your Lord is not one-eyed. Written between the eyes of the Dajjaal is Kaafir (disbeliever).” (Ahmad) Bukhaaree and Muslim related it from Sho’bah.

A hadith From Safeenah

Safeenah, a freed slave of the Messenger of Allah # , said, “The Messenger of Allah 3% gave us a sermon and said, ‘Lo! Verily, there was no Prophet before me except that he warned his nation about the Dajjaal; he is ‘Aawar (one-eyed) from the right eye. On his right eye is coarse skin, and written between his eyes is Kaafir (disbeliever). Two valleys will come out with him; one of them is his paradise and the other is his fire: in reality, his fire is paradise and his paradise is fire. With him are two angels who resemble two Prophets; had I wished to give their names (ie. of the two Prophets) and the name of their fathers, I would have done so. One of them is on his right, and the other is on his left, and that is Fituah (trial). The Dajjaal will say: ‘Am I not your Lord? Do I not give life and cause death” One of the two angels will say: ‘You have lied.’ But none of the people will hear him; only his companion (the other ange!) hears him, and he will respond: ‘You have spoken the truth,’ The people will hear him and think that he is approving of and confirming the Dajjaal — and that is Fitna (trial). He will continue moving until he tries to enter Al-Madeenah, but permission to enter it will not be given to him. He will say: ‘This is the village of that man.’ Then he will continue to travel until he reaches Ash-Sham, and Allah will destroy him at ‘Aqabah Afeeq.” (Ahmad) Ahmad alone related it; its chain is okay but the text has Gharaabah and Nakaarah in it. And Allah % knows best.

The hadith Of Mu’aadh Ibn Jabal

Abu Lailah ibn Abee Umayyah related that a group of people entered upon Mu’aadh Ibn Jabal 4 when he was sick, They said to him, “Relate to us a hadith that you heard from the Messenger of

110 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

Samarah related this from the Prophet 2: “The Dajjaal is ‘Aawar (oneeyed) from the left eye; upon it is coarse skin.” This narration is mentioned by Adh-Dhahabee in his book about the Dajjaal. However, this hadith with this chain is not in the Musnad, nor is it in any of the Six Books. And it would have been more worthy of the Shaikh to give, its chain or to ascribe it to a well-known book, And Allah 4 is the granter of success.

A hadith Related From Samarah Ibn Janaadah Ibn Jundub &

Tha’‘labah ibn ‘Ibaad Al-‘Abdee, from the people of Basrah, said, “One day, I attended the Khutbah of Samarah. In it, he mentioned a hadith about the eclipse prayer. He mentioned that the Messenger of Allah 28 gave a sermon after the eclipse prayer, and in it he # said, ‘By Allah, the Hour will not arrive until 30 come out, the last of them being the One-Eyed Dajjaal, whose left eye is effaced, as if it is the eye of Abee Yahyaa. When he comes out — or whenever he comes out — he will indeed claim that he is Allah. Whoever has faith in him, believes in him, and follows him, his past good deeds will not benefit him. And whoever disbelieves in him and rejects him will not be punished for any of his deeds - Al-Hasan said, ‘for any of his past deeds’ — and he will appear on all of the earth except for the Haram and Jerusalem. And he will besiege the believers in Jerusalem. They will be shaken violently and then Allah will destroy him, Then the base of a garden and the root of a tree will call out: O believer, this is a Jew — and this is a disbeliever — so kill him. But

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 11

that will not come to pass like that until you see matters intensify in significance within your own selves, and you will ask one another — did your Prophet mention to you some of those matters...” (Ahmad) Tha’labah then attended another Khutbah of Samarah, and he repeated the exact same words, without changing anything in the least. The basis for this hadith is with the compilers of the Four Sunan. At-Tirmidhee ruled it to be authentic; Ibn Hibbaan related it as well and Al-Haakim related it in his Mustadrak.
content_id:
Berbagai Hadits tentang Dajjal

Hadith Abu Bakr As-Shiddiq #

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 107

seperti perisai-perisai, akan mengikutinya.'" (Ahmad) Ibn Maajah dan At-Tirmidhee meriwayatkannya dari Rooh ibn 'Ubaadah, dan At-Tirmidhee berkata, "Hasan sahih."

Hadith 'Alee Ibn Abee Taalib #

'Alee # berkata, "Kami menyebutkan Dajjal di hadapan Nabi #% sementara beliau sedang tidur. Beliau terbangun, memerah wajahnya, dan berkata, 'Selain itu, aku lebih khawatir tentang kalian.'" (Ahmad)

Hadith Sa'ad Ibn Abee Waqqaas #

Sa'ad meriwayatkan dari Maalik dari ayahnya dari ayahnya, bahwa Rasulullah #% berkata, "Setiap Nabi menggambarkan Dajjal kepada umatnya, dan aku akan menggambarkan satu sifatnya yang tidak pernah digambarkan oleh siapa pun sebelumku (mengenainya). Ia sungguh-sungguh buta sebelah, dan Allah 'Azza wa-Jall (kepada-Nya milik Kemuliaan dan Keagungan) tidak buta sebelah." (Ahmad)

Hadith Abu 'Ubaidah Ibn Al-Jarraah #

Abu 'Ubaidah ibn Al-Jarraah meriwayatkan bahwa ia mendengar Rasulullah #% berkata, "Sesungguhnya, tidak ada Nabi kecuali ia memperingatkan kaumnya tentang Dajjal, dan aku memperingatkan kalian tentang dia." Abu 'Ubaidah berkata, "Rasulullah #% kemudian menggambarkannya kepada kami dan berkata, 'Mungkin sebagian yang melihat (aku) dan mendengar ucapanku akan mendapatinya (yaitu akan masih hidup ketika ia keluar).'" Para sahabat # berkata, "Wahai Rasulullah, bagaimana keadaan hati kami pada saat itu?" Beliau #% berkata, "Sama — sebagaimana hari ini — atau lebih baik." Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmidhee, yang berkata bahwa hadith itu Hasan. Abu Daawood juga meriwayatkannya, dan Ahmad meriwayatkan sebagiannya.

'Abdullah Ibn Khabbaab mendengarkan Ubai Ibn Ka'ab # ketika ia meriwayatkan dari Rasulullah #%. Ketika Dajjal disebutkan di hadapannya, ia berkata, "Seolah-olah salah satu dari dua matanya adalah kaca. Dan berlindunglah kepada Allah dari azab kubur." (Ahmad)

108 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar

sesungguhnya Penutup dari 1000 atau lebih (Nabi). Tidak ada seorang Nabi yang diikuti yang diutus kecuali ia memperingatkan umatnya tentang Dajjal, dan suatu perkara sungguh-sungguh telah diperjelaskan kepadaku, yang sebelumnya belum pernah diperjelaskan kepada siapapun. Ia buta sebelah dan sesungguhnya Tuhanmu tidak buta sebelah. Mata kanannya adalah 'Aura (cacat); ia tidak tersembunyi tetapi malah menonjol secara tidak normal, seolah-olah itu adalah dahak di dinding berlapis. Dan mata kirinya seperti bintang yang berkilauan. Bersamanya ada setiap bahasa dan bersamanya ada gambaran surga, dengan tanaman hijau; dan di dalamnya air mengalir; dan (bersamanya ada) gambaran neraka, berwarna hitam dan mengeluarkan asap." (Ahmad)

Sebuah Hadith yang Diriwayatkan dari Anas Ibn Maalik #

Anas ibn Maalik # meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah #% berkata, "Dajjal akan datang dan menginjak setiap bagian bumi kecuali Makkah dan Al-Madeenah. Ia akan datang ke Al-Madeenah dan menemukan di setiap celah pegunungannya barisan-barisan malaikat. Kemudian ia akan pergi ke sisi tanah yang gersang dan asin, dan ia akan mendirikan tendanya (atau tempat duduknya). Al-Madeenah akan berguncang tiga kali, dan setiap orang munafik laki-laki dan perempuan akan keluar darinya dan pergi kepadanya." (Ahmad) Muslim juga meriwayatkannya, tetapi melalui sanad lain.

Sanad Lain dari Anas

Anas # meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi #% berkata, "Sesungguhnya Dajjal buta sebelah, dari (mata) kiri, di atasnya ada kulit kasar. Tertulis di antara matanya adalah Kafara (kata kerja lampau untuk kekafiran) atau Kaafir (kekafiran)." (Ahmad) Riwayat ini memenuhi syarat-syarat Bukhaaree dan Muslim.

Sanad Lain dari Anas

Anas ibn Maalik # meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah #¥ berkata, "Dajjal akan keluar dari Yahudi Asbahaan; bersamanya ada 70.000 orang Yahudi, dan atas mereka ada mahkota-mahkota." (Ahmad) Dan Anas meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah # berkata, "Dajjal memiliki mata yang terhapus, dan di antara matanya tertulis Kaafir (orang kafir)." Kemudian Nabi # mengeja huruf-huruf (dari Kaafir): Ka-Fa-Ra. Beliau #¢ kemudian berkata, "Setiap Muslim akan membacanya." (Ahmad) Dan dalam riwayat lain, Anas ibn Maalik # meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah #% berkata, "Dajjal buta sebelah, dan sesungguhnya Tuhanmu tidak buta sebelah. Tertulis di antara mata Dajjal adalah Kaafir, yang akan dibaca oleh setiap

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 109

orang yang beriman, baik ia melek huruf maupun buta huruf." (Ahmad) Muslim juga meriwayatkannya, tetapi melalui sanad lain.

Sanad Lain Lagi dari Anas #

Anas # meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah #% berkata, "Tidak ada seorang Nabi pun yang diutus kecuali ia memperingatkan umatnya tentang Pendusta Buta Sebelah; kecuali ia sungguh-sungguh buta sebelah, dan Tuhanmu tidak buta sebelah. Tertulis di antara mata Dajjal adalah Kaafir (orang kafir)." (Ahmad) Bukhaaree dan Muslim meriwayatkannya dari Shu'bah.

Sebuah Hadith dari Safeenah

Safeenah, mantan budak Rasulullah #%, berkata, "Rasulullah #% memberikan khutbah kepada kami dan berkata, 'Ketahuilah! Sungguh tidak ada Nabi sebelumku kecuali ia memperingatkan umatnya tentang Dajjal; ia 'Aawar (buta sebelah) dari mata kanan. Di mata kanannya ada kulit kasar, dan tertulis di antara matanya adalah Kaafir (orang kafir). Dua lembah akan keluar bersamanya; salah satunya adalah surganya dan yang lain adalah nerakanya: pada kenyataannya, nerakanya adalah surga dan surganya adalah neraka. Bersamanya ada dua malaikat yang menyerupai dua Nabi; seandainya aku ingin menyebutkan nama mereka (yaitu dua Nabi) dan nama bapak-bapak mereka, aku sudah melakukannya. Salah satu dari keduanya ada di sisi kanannya, dan yang lain ada di sisi kirinya, dan itulah Fitnah (cobaan). Dajjal akan berkata: "Apakah aku bukan Tuhanmu? Apakah aku tidak memberikan kehidupan dan menyebabkan kematian?" Salah satu dari dua malaikat itu akan berkata: "Kamu telah berdusta." Tetapi tidak ada seorang pun dari manusia yang mendengarnya; hanya temannya (malaikat lain) yang mendengarnya, dan ia akan merespons: "Kamu telah berkata benar." Orang-orang akan mendengarnya dan mengira bahwa ia menyetujui dan mengkonfirmasi Dajjal — dan itulah Fitnah (cobaan). Ia akan terus bergerak hingga ia mencoba memasuki Al-Madeenah, tetapi izin untuk memasukinya tidak akan diberikan kepadanya. Ia akan berkata: "Ini adalah desa laki-laki itu." Kemudian ia akan terus bepergian hingga ia mencapai Syam, dan Allah akan menghancurkannya di 'Aqabah Afeeq.'" (Ahmad) Ahmad sendirian yang meriwayatkannya; sanadnya baik tetapi teksnya mengandung Gharaabah dan Nakaarah. Dan Allah #% Maha Mengetahui.

Hadith Mu'aadh Ibn Jabal

Abu Lailah ibn Abee Umayyah meriwayatkan bahwa sekelompok orang masuk menemui Mu'aadh Ibn Jabal # ketika ia sedang sakit. Mereka berkata kepadanya, "Riwayatkanlah kepada kami sebuah hadith yang kamu dengar dari Rasulullah

110 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar

Samarah meriwayatkan ini dari Nabi #%: "Dajjal adalah 'Aawar (buta sebelah) dari mata kiri; di atasnya ada kulit kasar." Riwayat ini disebutkan oleh Adz-Dzahabee dalam kitabnya tentang Dajjal. Namun, hadith ini dengan sanad ini tidak ada dalam Musnad, dan juga tidak dalam salah satu dari Enam Kitab. Dan akan lebih layak bagi Syaikh untuk memberikan sanadnya atau menisbatkannya pada kitab yang terkenal. Dan Allah # adalah Pemberi Taufik.

Sebuah Hadith yang Diriwayatkan dari Samarah Ibn Janaadah Ibn Jundub #

Tha'labah ibn 'Ibaad Al-'Abdee, dari penduduk Basrah, berkata, "Suatu hari, aku menghadiri Khutbah Samarah. Di dalamnya, ia menyebutkan sebuah hadith tentang shalat gerhana. Ia menyebutkan bahwa Rasulullah #8 berkhutbah setelah shalat gerhana, dan di dalamnya beliau # berkata, 'Demi Allah, Hari Kiamat tidak akan tiba hingga 30 orang keluar, yang terakhir di antara mereka adalah Dajjal Buta Sebelah, yang mata kirinya terhapus, seolah-olah itu adalah mata Abee Yahyaa. Ketika ia keluar — atau kapan pun ia keluar — ia sungguh-sungguh akan mengklaim bahwa dirinya adalah Allah. Barangsiapa beriman kepadanya, mempercayainya, dan mengikutinya, amal-amal baiknya yang lalu tidak akan memberinya manfaat. Dan barangsiapa kafir kepadanya dan menolaknya tidak akan dihukum atas perbuatan-perbuatannya mana pun — Al-Hasan berkata, "atas perbuatan-perbuatan masa lalunya" — dan ia akan muncul di seluruh bumi kecuali Al-Haram dan Yerusalem. Dan ia akan mengepung orang-orang yang beriman di Yerusalem. Mereka akan diguncang dengan keras kemudian Allah akan menghancurkannya. Kemudian pangkal sebuah kebun dan akar sebuah pohon akan berseru: Wahai orang yang beriman, ini adalah seorang Yahudi — dan ini adalah orang kafir — maka bunuhlah dia. Tetapi

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 111

itu tidak akan terjadi seperti itu sampai kalian melihat perkara-perkara semakin intens maknanya dalam diri kalian sendiri, dan kalian akan saling bertanya — apakah Nabi kalian menyebutkan kepada kalian sebagian dari perkara-perkara itu..." (Ahmad) Tha'labah kemudian menghadiri Khutbah Samarah yang lain, dan ia mengulangi kata-kata yang persis sama, tanpa mengubah apapun sedikitpun. Dasar untuk hadith ini ada pada para pengompilasi Empat Sunan. At-Tirmidhee menilainya sahih; Ibn Hibbaan juga meriwayatkannya dan Al-Haakim meriwayatkannya dalam Mustadrak-nya.
content_ar: Various Ahaadeeth About The Dajjaal The الحديث Of أبو بكر As-Siddeeq - الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 107 like shields, will follow him.’” (أحمد) Ibn Maajah and AtTirmidhee related it from Rooh ibn ‘Ubaadah, and At-Tirmidhee said, “حسن صحيح.” The الحديث Of ‘Alee Ibn Abee Taalib 4 ‘Alee ts said, “We mentioned the Dajjaal in the presence of النبي ﷺ @ as he was sleeping. He woke up, red in color, and قال, “Other than that do I الخوف more upon you.” (أحمد) The الحديث Of Sa’ad Ibn Abee Waqqaas 4 Sa’ad related from Maalik from his الأب from his الأب, that الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% said, “Every single النبي described the Dajjaal to his nation, and I will describe a quality of his that no one قبل me has described (فيما يخص him). He is indeed one-eyed, and الله ‘Azza wa-Jall (to Him belongs Might and Majesty) is not one-eyed.” (أحمد) The الحديث Of Abu ‘Ubaidah Ibn Al-Jarraah 2 Abu ‘Ubaidah ibn Al-Jarraah related that he heard الرسول ﷺ of الله % say, “Indeed, there was never a النبي except that he warned his people about the Dafjaal, and I am warning you about him.” Abu ‘Ubaidah said, “الرسول ﷺ of الله #% then described him for us and said, ‘Perhaps some who see (me) and hear my speech will reach him (i.e., will be alive عندما he comes out).”" The الصحابة 4 said, “O Messenger of الله, how will our القلوب be at that time?” He #% said, “The same - as today - or better.” It is related by At-Tirmidhee, who said that it is حسن. Abu Daawood related it as well, and أحمد related a part of it. ‘Abdullah Ibn Khabbaab listened to Ubai Ibn Ka’ab & as he was relating from الرسول ﷺ of الله #. عندما the Dajjaal was mentioned in his presence, قال, “It is as إذا one of his two eyes is glass. And seek refuge in الله from the العقاب of the القبر.” (أحمد) 108 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations indeed the Seal of 1000 or more (الأنبياء). No followed النبي was sent except that he warned his nation about the Dajjaal, and a matter has indeed been made clear to me, which has not hitherto been made clear to anyone. He is one-eyed and indeed your Lord is not one-eyed. It is his الحق eye that is ‘Aouraa (defected); it is not concealed but rather protrudes abnormally, as إذا it is phlegm on a plastered wall. And his left eye is like a glistening star. With him is every language and with him is a picture of الجنة, with greenery; and in it water flows; and (with him is) a picture of the Fire, black and giving off smoke.” (أحمد) A الحديث Related From أنس Ibn Maalik 4 أنس ibn Maalik .% related that الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% said, “The Dajjaal will come and step on every part of the earth except for مكة المكرمة and Al-Madeenah. He will come to Al-Madeenah and find at each of its mountain-passes rows of الملائكة. Then he will go the side of a land that is barren and salty, and he will strike his tent (or place where he is seated). Al-Madeenah will shake three times, and every male and female hypocrite will come out of it and go to him.” (أحمد) مسلم related it as well, but through another chain. Another Chain From أنس أنس 4 related that النبي ﷺ ## said, “Indeed the Dajjaal is oneeyed, from the left (eye), over which is coarse skin. Written between his eyes is Kafara (the past-tense verb for disbelief) or Kaafir (cisbelief)."” (أحمد) This narration fulfills the conditions of Bukhaaree and مسلم. Another Chain From أنس أنس ibn Maalik . related that الرسول ﷺ of الله #¥ said, “The Dajjaal will come out from the Jews of Asbahaan; with him are 70,000 Jews, and upon them are crowns.” (أحمد) And أنس related that الرسول ﷺ of الله x said, “The Dajjaal has an eye that is effaced, and between his eyes is written Kaafir (disbeliever).”” Then النبي ﷺ # spoke the letters (of Kaafir): Ka-Fa-Ra. He #¢ then said, “Every مسلم will read it.” (أحمد) And in another narration, أنس ibn Maalik 4s related that الرسول ﷺ of الله 2 said, “The Dajjaal is one-eyed, and indeed, your Lord is not oneeyed. Written between the eyes of the Dajjaal is Kaafir, which every الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations ‘109 المؤمن will read, regardless of whether he is literate or illiterate.” (Almad) مسلم related it as well, but through another chain. Yet Another Chain From أنس 4 أنس .& related that الرسول ﷺ of الله 2% said, “No النبي ‘was sent except that he warned his nation about the One-Eyed Liar; except he is indeed one-eyed, and your Lord is not one-eyed. Written between the eyes of the Dajjaal is Kaafir (disbeliever).” (أحمد) Bukhaaree and مسلم related it from Sho’bah. A الحديث From Safeenah Safeenah, a freed slave of الرسول ﷺ of الله # , said, “الرسول ﷺ of الله 3% gave us a sermon and said, ‘Lo! Verily, there was no النبي قبل me except that he warned his nation about the Dajjaal; he is ‘Aawar (one-eyed) from the الحق eye. On his الحق eye is coarse skin, and written between his eyes is Kaafir (disbeliever). Two valleys will come out with him; one of them is his الجنة and the other is his fire: in reality, his fire is الجنة and his الجنة is fire. With him are two الملائكة who resemble two الأنبياء; had I wished to give their names (ie. of the two الأنبياء) and the name of their fathers, I would have done لذا. One of them is on his الحق, and the other is on his left, and that is Fituah (الابتلاء). The Dajjaal will say: ‘Am I not your Lord? Do I not give الحياة and cause الموت” One of the two الملائكة will say: ‘You have lied.’ But none of the people will hear him; only his companion (the other ange!) hears him, and he will respond: ‘You have spoken the الحق,’ The people will hear him and think that he is approving of and confirming the Dajjaal — and that is Fitna (الابتلاء). He will continue moving حتى he tries to enter Al-Madeenah, but permission to enter it will not be given to him. He will say: ‘This is the village of that man.’ Then he will continue to travel حتى he reaches Ash-Sham, and الله will destroy him at ‘Aqabah Afeeq.” (أحمد) أحمد alone related it; its chain is okay but the text has Gharaabah and Nakaarah in it. And الله % knows best. The الحديث Of Mu’aadh Ibn Jabal Abu Lailah ibn Abee Umayyah related that a group of people entered upon Mu’aadh Ibn Jabal 4 عندما he was sick, قالوا to him, “Relate to us a الحديث that you heard from الرسول ﷺ of 110 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations Samarah related this from النبي ﷺ 2: “The Dajjaal is ‘Aawar (oneeyed) from the left eye; upon it is coarse skin.” This narration is mentioned by Adh-Dhahabee in his الكتاب about the Dajjaal. غير أن, this الحديث with this chain is not in the Musnad, nor is it in any of the Six الكتب. And it would have been more worthy of the Shaikh to give, its chain or to ascribe it to a well-known الكتاب, And الله 4 is the granter of الفلاح. A الحديث Related From Samarah Ibn Janaadah Ibn Jundub & Tha’‘labah ibn ‘Ibaad Al-‘Abdee, from the people of Basrah, said, “يوماً ما, I attended the Khutbah of Samarah. In it, ذكر a الحديث about the eclipse الصلاة. ذكر that الرسول ﷺ of الله 28 gave a sermon بعد the eclipse الصلاة, and in it he # said, ‘By الله, the Hour will not arrive حتى 30 come out, the last of them being the One-Eyed Dajjaal, whose left eye is effaced, as إذا it is the eye of Abee Yahyaa. عندما he comes out — or كلما he comes out — he will indeed claim that he is الله. Whoever has الإيمان in him, believes in him, and follows him, his past الخير deeds will not benefit him. And whoever disbelieves in him and rejects him will not be punished for any of his deeds - Al-حسن said, ‘for any of his past deeds’ — and he will appear on all of the earth except for the الحرام and Jerusalem. And he will besiege the المؤمنون in Jerusalem. They will be shaken violently and then الله will destroy him, Then the base of a garden and the root of a tree will call out: O المؤمن, this is a Jew — and this is a disbeliever — لذا kill him. But الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 11 that will not come to pass like that حتى you see matters intensify in significance within your own selves, and you will ask بعضهم بعضاً — did your النبي mention to you some of those matters...” (أحمد) Tha’labah then attended another Khutbah of Samarah, and he repeated the exact same words, without changing anything in the least. The basis for this الحديث is with the compilers of the Four Sunan. At-Tirmidhee ruled it to be authentic; Ibn Hibbaan related it as well and Al-Haakim related it in his Mustadrak.
